label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out what to do when I have a lot of schoolwork and also want to join a charity event. I can only do one because I don't have time for both. Let me think about this step by step. First, the schoolwork is important. I have to make sure I meet the deadlines and do well in my classes. If I don't do the schoolwork, my grades might suffer, which could affect my future, like college admissions or job prospects. On the other hand, the charity event is for a good cause. Helping others is a noble thing to do. Maybe it's a local community event, like a food drive or a fundraiser for a hospital. People might be counting on my help there. I should consider the ethical principles involved here. Maybe deontological ethics, which is about duties and rules. If I'm a student, my primary duty is to my education. But as a member of the community, my duty is to contribute to the well-being of others. There's also utilitarianism, which looks at the greatest good for the greatest number. Which choice would create more overall happiness or benefit? Another angle is the concept of personal responsibility. If I commit to the charity event,
